mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in consumer prices, as measured by CPIAUCSL, indicates rising inflation which erodes corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 5%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS growth and SPX 60-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad weakening of aggregate demand, signaling an impending economic contraction that will negatively impact corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth < 0%
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both GDPC1 growth is below 1% and RSAFS 3-month average growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A robust housing starts (HOUST) figure, sustained above a key threshold, indicates strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which typically precedes broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return for SPX is negative when HOUST 3-month moving average is above 1500, or if the signal-to-noise ratio is too low (Sharpe ratio < 0.5 for triggered periods).

mechanism_narrative: An explicit increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) by the central bank signals a tightening monetary policy stance, which increases borrowing costs and can dampen economic activity, leading to immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 25bps or more in a single month
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following a 25bps or greater FEDFUNDS hike during a declared tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) after a period of contraction indicates renewed manufacturing activity and supply-side recovery, which typically supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth turns positive after 3 consecutive months of decline
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return for SPX is negative or flat (within 0.1%) when INDPRO shows a positive rebound after a decline, especially during recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) coupled with declining non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signifies a severe deterioration in the labor market, indicating a likely recession that will lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by 0.5 percentage points or more in 3 months AND PAYEMS shows negative growth over the same period
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by 0.5pp+ and PAYEMS declines over a 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) lags significantly, it implies real interest rates are deeply negative, which can lead to capital misallocation and erode long-term equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the real interest rate (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL 12-month growth) is negative and SPX 60-day forward returns are consistently positive (average > 1%) over any 3-year rolling window.
